methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $500M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Collaborative Robotics, founded by Brad Porter, is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company currently valued at $500M+ post-money, having raised $150M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 comparable companies: 1 public company and 8 private firms at equivalent or nearby stages, including 1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Fairmat, and GrayMatter Robotics.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $500M last post-money valuation as a base and applying a 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier, implies a forward valuation of $950M. Peer-median benchmarking across five same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $1.6B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. The wide peer dispersion reflects heterogeneity in robotics company maturity, application specificity, and capital efficiency.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $808M to $1.9B, with a median of $1.3B. The current $500M+ valuation sits below both implied values, suggesting potential upside to fair value in the $1.2B–$1.6B band, though significant uncertainty remains around the appropriate peer weighting given the $10.0B outlier (1X Technologies) and the $300M floor. Key variables affecting this range include deployment traction, gross margins, and whether frontier-stage risk justifies peer-relative discounts or premiums.
